description_long: This is a mid-rise apartment building with a rectangular footprint, constructed primarily from reddish-brown brick. The facade features five vertical rows of large, dark-framed windows, with four rows of balconies on one side, each balcony enclosed by metal railings. The building has a flat, concrete roof and a symmetrical window arrangement, giving it a utilitarian and modern appearance. The ground floor includes two main entrances, each with a recessed doorway. The side and rear elevations are simpler, with only windows and no balconies. The overall design is functional, typical of urban residential blocks built for efficiency and density.
description: A rectangular, brick apartment building with multiple balconies, large windows, and a flat roof, designed for urban residential use.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main facade with five rows of windows, four rows of balconies, and two ground-level entrances.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a plain brick wall with vertically aligned windows and no balconies.
description_view_2: Rear cutaway view revealing the interior floor structure and the alignment of balconies on one side.
description_view_3: Angled side view highlighting the balconies and window arrangement along the building's side.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in a symmetrical grid pattern, with evenly spaced vertical and horizontal alignment across all visible facades.
